core_mechanism: geometric-phase (holonomy) transport: net directed displacement produced by a closed, non-reciprocal cycle in a low-dimensional shape/control space under overdamped, inertia-free linear coupling, where the drift per cycle is a loop integral over the control parameters (enclosed-area rule)
brief_reason: The abstract computes swimming velocity, force-velocity response, and efficiency for cyclic deformations of a two-arm (two-parameter) body in the inertialess low-Reynolds regime, so the net motion arises purely from the geometry of the closed stroke cycle in shape space rather than from any accumulated momentum.
